top10Blockers.fix: lib/api/fund24.ts:36 — change endpoint to /api/foerdermittel/katalog/:id. 1-line fix unlocks 3.408 programme detail pages.
middleware.ts:23 — add '/antraege' to PROTECTED_PREFIXES. 1-line fix closes an auth leak.
app/sitemap.ts:34 and :52 — read data.data and data.berater (or verify actual response shape). Closes a big SEO loss.
app/(public)/foerder-schnellcheck/bericht/page.tsx:151 — split consent + marketing into two checkboxes; add link to /datenschutz.
worker/src/routes/check.ts — add requireAuth to 5 endpoints; current state drains AI+R2 quota.
worker/src/routes/admin.ts:261 — add host allowlist; disable redirect:'follow' for foerderprogramme.url checks.
Decide canonical 'users' table. Consolidate writes and reads to one DB. Multi-week migration.
app/dashboard/berater/profil/page.tsx — include spezialisierungen + websiteUrl in update payload.
Either create /dashboard/checks/[id] or redirect 'Ansehen' to an existing detail route; fix /programme/[id] 404 (see #1).
Remove ComingSoonBanner from admin + dashboard/berater + unternehmen subpage layouts — features already ship.

findings.summary: All IDs return 404 — getProgramm() calls non-existent endpoint /api/foerderprogramme/:id; correct path is /api/foerdermittel/katalog/:id.
Served 200 without authentication; missing from middleware PROTECTED_PREFIXES. Only a post-hydration client-side guard.
0 programme URLs emitted because fetchProgrammIds() reads wrong response field.
findings.fix: app/sitemap.ts:25-43 — map from data.data not data.results

findings.summary: Silent data loss: form captures spezialisierungen + websiteUrl but the update payload omits them.
Dead link: 'Ansehen' → /dashboard/checks/[id] — route does not exist in app/.
findings.fix: Redirect to /foerdercheck/[sessionId]/ergebnisse or create the route

findings.summary: Single checkbox bundles Datenschutz-Consent and newsletter opt-in → DSGVO issue; no link to /datenschutz.
No <h1> on page; uses <h2> for active question. A11y + single-h1 convention.
Uses native browser alert() on success despite sonner Toaster being configured.
ComingSoonBanner wraps entire subtrees even for functioning pages (berater dashboard, admin provisionen, aktuelles) — UX friction.
Branche taxonomy mismatch — 11 entries with amp-suffix vs 10 short-form entries. Matching logic will never line up.
Native alert()/confirm() on /vorlagen, /admin/users, /berater/[id] vs sonner Toaster + custom dialogs elsewhere.
router.push() called during render, not in useEffect — hydration warnings.
Hardcoded '9,99%' in column header despite per-row provisionsSatz; edit writes structured 'bewilligt: X EUR' into free-text notiz field.
Partial-failure risk: sequential for-await loop persists entry 1-2, silently drops 4-5 if 3 fails.
Light-theme gradient styling leaks into authenticated wizard while rest of app uses the dark 'Institutional Architect' theme.
0 per-page metadata on 55 of 58 pages — only root metadata + /programme + /impressum + /foerder-schnellcheck layout declare any. All social-share previews use the generic fund24 title/description.
Program-count inconsistency: root metadata says '3.400+', /programme metadata says '2.500+'.

Typos and Denglisch across public + authenticated pages: 'Konteniverwaltung', 'behhalten', 'behaften', 'Zahlte Provisionen', 'Förderbereichcen', 'aller Ihre Anfragen', 'matched' (should be 'matcht'), 'Was passiert nächster?', 'Newsletters'.
Lives in (public) route group instead of (auth); re-implements chrome inconsistent with the 4 other auth pages.
Names Berlin data-protection authority, but company is in Kronach (Bavaria, IHK Oberfranken Bayreuth).
Heading still 'Angaben gemäß § 5 Telemediengesetz (TMG)' though law was superseded by DDG.
'Letzte Aktualisierung: new Date().toLocaleDateString()' — always shows today, no real audit trail.
Support email inconsistency — info@fund24.io on /support, support@fund24.io in SupportWidget.
TODO banners at bottom: 'zu Demonstrationszwecken erstellt — bitte anwaltlich überarbeiten'.
Password regex mismatch — registrieren requires [!@#$%^&*], reset allows a broader set, so reset allows weaker-rule passwords.
No reset() wired up; Sentry tagging convention missing (inconsistent with app/error.tsx and /passwort-reset).
Design-system drift: /berater uses components/ui/select, /programme uses raw <select>.
Inkonsistenter Fördercheck-Pfad: /aktuelles/[slug] linkt auf /foerdercheck, alle anderen Seiten auf /foerder-schnellcheck
findings.evidence: <Link href="/foerdercheck" className=...>Fördercheck starten</Link> — Landing page (app/(public)/page.tsx:41) und Preise-Seite nutzen aber /foerder-schnellcheck als Einstiegspfad. Beide Routen existieren, was aber zu uneinheitlicher UX und SEO-Dopplung führen kann.

findings.summary: AGB- und Datenschutz-Seiten enthalten Demo-Disclaimer in Produktionscode
findings.evidence: Diese AGB sind zu Demonstrationszwecken erstellt. Bitte lassen Sie diese von einem Rechtsanwalt überarbeiten...' wird im Footer gerendert. Identische Notiz in datenschutz/page.tsx:282. Rechtliches Compliance-Risiko bei Live-Betrieb.

findings.summary: Passwort-reset Success-Screen (Link href=/login) kann nicht über href weiter zu Dashboard navigieren, sondern nur zum Login — Nutzer muss erneut credentials eingeben obwohl frisch reset
findings.evidence: Nach erfolgreichem Reset wird nur ein statischer Link zu /login angeboten; keine automatische Session-Erstellung. Das ist sicherheitstechnisch ok, aber UX-Friction.

findings.summary: Registrieren-Seite: nach 'requiresVerification' im legacy-Path Navigation zu /verifizieren ohne email-Query
findings.evidence: else { login(res.token, res.user); ... router.push('/verifizieren') } — verifizieren/page.tsx:31 redirected sofort zu /login wenn kein email in URL oder store ist. Fallback funktioniert nur wenn store-user bereits gesetzt ist. Fragiler Code-Pfad.

findings.summary: DSGVO consent bundling on /foerder-schnellcheck/bericht — single checkbox combines privacy-policy acceptance with marketing-email opt-in
findings.evidence: <Label>Ich akzeptiere die Datenschutzerklärung UND möchte E-Mails von fund24 erhalten</Label> — bundled into z.object({ dsgvo: z.boolean() }). TTDSG §25 / DSGVO Art. 7(2) require separate, specific consent for marketing emails vs. contract-based processing.

findings.summary: /programme/[id] is known-dead (H-P1-01): all outgoing links from /programme land on a 404
findings.evidence: await getProgramm(Number(id)) calls /api/foerderprogramme/{id} which 404s. notFound() fires for every ID, so 'Zurück zu Förderungen' and 'Zum Fördercheck' CTAs never render. ProgrammKarte links to /programme/{id} from /programme — entire detail path is broken.

findings.summary: router.push() called during render (React anti-pattern) on chat, ergebnis, bericht pages
findings.evidence: chat/page.tsx:25-28 and ergebnis/page.tsx:16-19 and bericht/page.tsx:46-49 all call router.push() synchronously in the component body (not in useEffect), then 'return null'. Next.js warns against this; it can cause 'Cannot update Router while rendering' errors and navigation race conditions. /profil/page.tsx correctly uses useEffect — inconsistent.

findings.summary: Native alert() used for success feedback on /berater/[id] Anfrage send — inconsistent with Toaster UX used elsewhere
findings.evidence: alert('Ihre Anfrage wurde erfolgreich gesendet!') — blocking browser dialog instead of the project's shadcn Sonner/Toaster component. Degrades UX and breaks design system consistency.

findings.summary: /foerder-schnellcheck does not read ?programm={id} query param sent from /programme/[id]
findings.evidence: The Fördercheck entry page (FoerderSchnellcheckPage) calls analysiereWebsite(data.url) with zero awareness of searchParams. The 'Zum Fördercheck' CTA from /programme/[id] passes ?programm={id} which is silently dropped — user intent lost. Also compounds with H-P1-01 since /programme/[id] never renders anyway.

findings.summary: No consent/DSGVO gate on website URL submission
findings.evidence: Form submits URL to analysiereWebsite() which scrapes the URL on the server with no user checkbox/consent that the URL will be processed + cached. Only the bericht (email) step has DSGVO. URL-scraping is processing of user-supplied data and should have a notice + checkbox at the entry point.

findings.summary: Inconsistent Select implementations between /berater (shadcn Select) and /programme (native HTML <select>)
findings.evidence: ProgrammListe uses native <select> elements with extensive Tailwind classes while /berater uses @/components/ui/select (shadcn). Visual and a11y behavior diverge across two sibling public pages.

findings.summary: Unauthenticated leak (H-P1-02): /antraege/[id] only protects with a post-hydration router.replace('/login') — the page shell renders with header/metadata before the effect runs. No server-side guard.
findings.evidence: useEffect(() => { if (!token) { router.replace('/login'); } }, [token, router]); — runs after hydration; no Next.js middleware/server redirect

findings.summary: PDF download CTA is a mock: clicking invokes alert('PDF-Download wird in Kürze implementiert'). Button is also `disabled` — user sees no feedback at all in normal state.
findings.evidence: const handleDownloadPDF = () => { alert('PDF-Download wird in Kürze implementiert') } ... <Button onClick={handleDownloadPDF} disabled><Download/>Ergebnisse als PDF</Button>

findings.summary: Partial-failure risk in onboarding expertise: sequential `for (const entry of data.entries) { await addExpertise(entry) }` persists entries 1..N-1 server-side if entry N fails, with no rollback or retry-from-N. Error state shows a generic message.
findings.evidence: for (const entry of data.entries) { await addExpertise(entry); } router.push('/onboarding/dienstleistungen')

findings.summary: Partial-failure risk in onboarding dienstleistungen: identical pattern. Client has no idempotency key / no 'already-added' deduplication — re-submit after partial failure would duplicate entries.
findings.evidence: for (const entry of data.entries) { await addDienstleistung(entry); } router.push('/dashboard/berater')

findings.summary: Inconsistent UX: DokumenteListe uses native browser confirm() for document delete — all other destructive actions in the Antrag detail (revoke ACL) use inline buttons with no confirmation.
findings.evidence: if (!confirm('Dokument wirklich löschen?')) return;

findings.summary: Berater-role onboarding pages (profil/expertise/dienstleistungen) rely only on client-side `istBerater()` checks inside useEffect. Unternehmen users hitting these URLs see the full page before redirect fires.
findings.evidence: useEffect(() => { if (!token) router.replace('/login'); if (nutzer && !istBerater()) router.replace('/dashboard/unternehmen'); }, ...)

findings.summary: Analyse page polls for up to 120s, then shows a fallback 'Zum Dashboard' button but no retry/refresh action — if backend status is slow, user must leave.
findings.evidence: {timeout ? (<Button onClick={() => router.push('/dashboard/unternehmen')}>Zum Dashboard</Button>) : ...}

findings.summary: Dead link to /dashboard/checks/[id] — route does not exist
findings.evidence: Link href=`/dashboard/checks/${check.id}`; glob app/dashboard/checks/** returns no files (confirms H-P1-05).

findings.summary: Silent data loss on Berater-Profil form (H-P1-04)
findings.evidence: Form collects spezialisierungen + websiteUrl (zod schema line 26–27) but updateBeraterProfil payload at lines 93–102 omits them — user edits are silently dropped.

findings.summary: Admin Provisionen uses free-text notiz field instead of structured columns, hardcoded 9,99% rate
findings.evidence: handleBetragChange serializes `bewilligt: ${betrag} EUR` into notiz (line 110); header label shows hardcoded 'Provision (9,99%)' (line 197); provisionBetrag is only updated in local state, never persisted server-side.

findings.summary: Native confirm() dialog used in Vorlagen delete
findings.evidence: `if (!confirm('Vorlage wirklich löschen?')) return;` — blocking native dialog, inconsistent with app-wide UI Dialog.

findings.summary: Native confirm() dialog used in Admin Users deactivate
findings.evidence: `if (!confirm('Diesen Nutzer wirklich deaktivieren?')) return` — bypasses shadcn Dialog / AlertDialog; inconsistent UX.

findings.summary: Dead 'Nachricht' button on unternehmen/anfragen (no onClick)
findings.evidence: <Button> for accepted Anfrage is rendered without any onClick handler; clicking does nothing.

findings.summary: Favoriten delete has no confirmation (destructive action)
findings.evidence: Trash button calls handleRemove(id) immediately — no confirm() or dialog guard for destructive favorite removal.

findings.summary: Berater dashboard labels 'Provisionen' but links to /abwicklung
findings.evidence: Button labelled 'Provisionen' navigates to /dashboard/berater/abwicklung — label/target mismatch.

findings.summary: Berater dashboard renders anfrage.nachricht under 'Dienstleistung:' label
findings.evidence:
Dienstleistung: {anfrage.nachricht || '-'}

— data-model mismatch, `nachricht` is free-text message, not service-id.

findings.summary: Bericht autosave uses onBlur — user losing focus in unusual flows may skip save
findings.evidence: All 5 fields call onBlur={handleBlur}; no debounced interval save. If user closes tab with focused field, changes may not persist (router.replace on create also relies on blur).

findings.summary: Audit-logs useCallback uses `offset` in deps but initial load only depends on `token`
findings.evidence: load callback deps include [filter, offset]; useEffect at line 74 ignores exhaustive-deps — filter changes via typing do not auto-reload (needs Filtern click); acceptable but brittle.

findings.summary: /api/check/* wizard is fully unauthenticated yet writes to BAFA_DB.foerdermittel_profile, uploads to R2 and consumes Workers AI on every request — abuse-vulnerable
findings.evidence: check.post('/', ...) — no requireAuth; only KV-session-id (caller-supplied) gates subsequent steps. Profile rows persist with user_id='check-{sessionId}'.

findings.summary: /api/checks/* proxy forwards anything (including body + Authorization header) to a separate Worker without any local auth or rate-limit on top of the global one
findings.evidence: forward() copies request to https://foerdermittel-check-api.froeba-kevin.workers.dev with browser-supplied headers; no token check on the Worker boundary

findings.summary: /api/admin/check-foerdermittel performs outbound HEAD requests to URLs read from foerderprogramme.url with only an 8s timeout — admin-triggered SSRF surface, mass-fan-out (50 per call)
findings.evidence: Promise.all over 50 fetch(p.url, {method: 'HEAD', redirect: 'follow'}) with no host allowlist; redirect=follow lets attackers hop to internal addresses if any malicious url ever lands in the table

findings.summary: /api/reports/preview/:id and /api/reports/download/:token return raw HTML from generated content without explicit XSS sanitization — content is AI-generated
findings.evidence: new Response(generateReportPreview(...), { 'Content-Type': 'text/html' }) — relies on report-html service to escape; no Content-Security-Policy header set on this response

findings.summary: Stripe webhook returns 200 with {received: true, error: '...'} on amount-mismatch and missing-payment cases — Stripe will not retry, log entry only in DB
findings.evidence: lines 100-114 return c.json({received:true, error:...}) instead of 4xx; Stripe treats this as accepted

findings.summary: /api/auth/refresh has no per-IP/per-user rate limit (only the global one); refresh-token rotation is the primary credential-recovery path
findings.evidence: POST /refresh has no loginRateLimit-style guard

findings.summary: /api/me/* uses sub-app .fetch() dispatch which re-enters the foerdermittel/unternehmen sub-app but bypasses the trailing-slash redirect + global pre-mount middleware that already ran on the outer request — works today but is fragile if middleware order changes
findings.evidence: forward() builds a new Request(targetUrl, c.req.raw) and calls foerdermittel.fetch(...) directly

findings.summary: /api/admin/users PATCH allows updating email without re-verifying — admin can silently take over accounts; no audit log shows old vs new email
findings.evidence: allowed list contains 'email'; updateProfile writes directly; audit log just records 'profile update {id}: email'

findings.summary: /api/foerdermittel/cases (POST) auto-creates a foerdermittel_profile from unternehmen with no rate-limit and triggers Workers-AI funnel generation (3000 max_tokens) per program — costly per-call
findings.evidence: ai.run('@cf/meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct-fp8', { max_tokens: 3000 }) on first case creation per program; only the global rate-limit guards

findings.summary: /api/foerdermittel/match runs LLM scoring in batches of 10 with no per-user concurrency cap; can exhaust Workers AI quota if hammered
findings.evidence: for-loop over chunks of 10 with ai.run(...) max_tokens=2000; only guarded by globalRateLimit

findings.summary: /api/admin/news DELETE is hard-delete — no soft-delete or audit log of the deletion
findings.evidence: DELETE FROM news_articles WHERE id = ?; no writeAuditLog call

findings.summary: PATCH /api/admin/provisionen/:id uses dynamic `${k} = ?` SQL, but keys come from zod-parsed object keys (safe). Still, pattern is duplicated across admin/tracker/reports — central helper would reduce drift risk
findings.evidence: for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(d)) sets.push(`${k} = ?`)

findings.summary: Multiple endpoints leak DB-shape error strings in non-production (onError returns err.message) — fine, but verify ENVIRONMENT is set in dev preview deployments to avoid leaking stack info
findings.evidence: error: c.env.ENVIRONMENT === 'production' ? 'Interner Serverfehler' : err.message

findings.summary: /api/verify-payment is unauthenticated — only correlates by sessionId/paymentId. Anyone with a leaked stripe session id could observe payment status (low-impact, no PII beyond reportTitle)
findings.evidence: verifyPayment.post('/verify-payment', async (c) => ...) — no requireAuth

findings.summary: /api/oa/* exposes operational/health metrics publicly (KV-cached CP/VA reports). Likely intentional, but worth confirming nothing-sensitive in those reports
findings.evidence: no requireAuth on any oa.* handler

findings.summary: GAP-001 cert-approve loop registers two routes via shared closure ([approve|reject]) on a single `for ... of` — callable, but Hono line numbers all map to admin.ts:446. Re-check unit tests cover both paths
findings.evidence: for (const [action, next] of [['approve','approved'],['reject','rejected']] as const) { admin.post(`/bafa-cert/:userId/${action}`, ...) }

findings.summary: `scrapeCompanyFromUrl` (called from /api/check) fetches a user-supplied URL. Currently no documented allowlist; SSRF-relevant for an unauthenticated endpoint
findings.evidence: if (body.url) { const scraped = await scrapeCompanyFromUrl(body.url); }

findings.summary: Role-check in /api/admin/users/:id/role only allows 'user'|'admin' — system has 'berater' and 'unternehmen' roles too. Promoting/demoting between those is not exposed (could be intentional)
findings.evidence: if (!['user','admin'].includes(role)) return ...

findings.summary: Several endpoints use `as any` / `as unknown as AiRunner` to cast the Workers-AI binding; safe today but masks future Workers-AI typed breakage (check.ts and inside services)
findings.evidence: type AiRunner = { run(model: string, options: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<any> }; (await (ai as unknown as AiRunner).run(...))

findings.summary: /api/antraege/:id PATCH and other endpoints email owners on phase change with executionCtx.waitUntil — failures swallowed silently; consider failure metric
findings.evidence: c.executionCtx.waitUntil(sendAntragStatusChangedEmail(...))

findings.summary: getProgramm() calls /api/foerderprogramme/:id but worker exposes /api/foerdermittel/katalog/:id — every /programme/[id] deep link 404s live (confirmed in 01_pages.json: flags BROKEN-all-ids-return-404).
findings.evidence: return apiCall<Foerderprogramm>(API.FUND24, `/api/foerderprogramme/${id}`)  // should be /api/foerdermittel/katalog/${id} — see app/(public)/programme/[id]/opengraph-image.tsx:15 which uses the correct path

findings.summary: getProgramme() list also targets /api/foerderprogramme which is not the worker's current listing route (worker uses /api/foerdermittel/katalog, matching sitemap.ts:30 and OG image). High suspicion /programme listing calls a route that does not exist.
findings.evidence: return apiCall<ProgrammeResponse>(API.FUND24, `/api/foerderprogramme${qs ? `?${qs}` : ''}`)

findings.summary: app/sitemap.ts:34 reads `data.results` but the katalog worker returns `{success, data, pagination}` — sitemap will always be empty in prod (known issue, still flagged).
findings.evidence: const data = (await res.json()) as { results?: KatalogItem[]; total?: number }  // actual worker response uses data: […]

findings.summary: lib/api/check.ts contains massive legacy surface (news, admin, tracker, favoriten, abwicklung, dashboard) pointing at API.CHECK (NEXT_PUBLIC_CHECK_API_URL) that is almost entirely shadowed by fund24.ts on API.FUND24. Candidates for either deletion or accidental use of the wrong worker (e.g. getDashboard, getNachrichten, sendeNachricht still used by pages).
findings.evidence: getDashboard → apiCall(API.CHECK, `/api/dashboard/${rolle}`, …) called from /dashboard/unternehmen & /dashboard/berater. CHECK base may point to legacy worker / stale URL.

findings.summary: getNachrichten + sendeNachricht still target API.CHECK at /api/netzwerk/nachrichten — used by /dashboard/berater/nachrichten. If CHECK points to foerdermittel-check-api (precheck) worker this route almost certainly does not exist; nachrichten page may be non-functional.
findings.evidence: apiCall(API.CHECK, `/api/netzwerk/nachrichten?anfrageId=${anfrageId}`, undefined, token)

findings.summary: app/sitemap.ts:52 reads `data.profiles` from /api/netzwerk/berater; the app-side wrapper (check.ts:getBeraterListe) types the response as { berater: BeraterProfil[] }. Shape between sitemap and wrapper is inconsistent — one (or both) is wrong.
findings.evidence: return (data.profiles ?? []).map((b) => b.id)  // but wrapper expects {berater: […]}

findings.summary: createAntrag wrapper normalizes three different response shapes ({data:{caseId}}, {case:{id}}, {id}) — indicates historical API drift that is silently hidden; any change in worker response breaks the fallback.
findings.evidence: return { id: r.data?.caseId ?? r.case?.id ?? r.id ?? '' }

findings.summary: Many exports in lib/api/* are never imported by any page/component (dead surface): getStats, verifyEmail, deleteDokument, deleteAdminNews, listExpertise, listDienstleistungen, getUnternehmen, createTrackerVorgang, deleteTrackerVorgang, addFavorit (fund24.ts). Reduces clarity and grows attack surface; all still go over production Bearer tokens if invoked.
findings.evidence: getStats (@6), verifyEmail (auth.ts@103), deleteDokument (@378), deleteAdminNews (@459), listExpertise (berater.ts@123), listDienstleistungen (berater.ts@204), getUnternehmen (unternehmen.ts@57), createTrackerVorgang (@488), deleteTrackerVorgang (@513), addFavorit (@57)

findings.summary: updateAnfrage wrapper silently coerces any unrecognized status to 'abgelehnt' to keep compatibility with legacy AnfrageStatus union — can result in reject actions happening on user's behalf if UI passes an unexpected status string.
findings.evidence: const mapped: 'angenommen' | 'abgelehnt' = status === 'angenommen' ? 'angenommen' : 'abgelehnt'

findings.summary: All authenticated fund24.ts helpers resolve the token via useAuth.getState().token at call time and silently pass null on SSR (typeof window === 'undefined' branch). If any of these helpers is ever invoked from a server component/generateMetadata it will issue an unauthenticated request and produce either a 401 or an anonymous response.
findings.evidence: function token(): string | null { if (typeof window === 'undefined') return null; return useAuth.getState().token }

findings.summary: updateAdminProvision wrapper widens status to plain string so 'invalid values get a 400 from the worker' — i.e. the UI relies on the worker for enum validation rather than enforcing client-side. Increases likelihood of unguarded state transitions shipping to admin UI.
findings.evidence: // Widened to string so the UI can pass its own ProvisionStatus type; invalid values get a 400 from the worker.

findings.summary: Duplicate getTracker/updateTrackerPhase/getNews/getNewsArtikel/getAdminDashboard/getAdminUsers/getAdminProvisionen/getAdminNews/createAdminNews/updateAdminNews/getProvisionVertraege/uploadAbwicklungDokument live in both check.ts and fund24.ts with different auth bases. Name collisions could produce subtle bugs if an import is ever re-pointed.
findings.evidence: getTracker (check.ts@134 on API.CHECK) vs getTracker (fund24.ts@482 on API.FUND24) — both named the same, exported from different files.

findings.summary: auth.ts:logout manually clears a document.cookie named 'fund24-auth' but the real HttpOnly cookie set by /api/session is 'fund24-token'. Client-side cookie clear is a no-op on the wrong name (plus HttpOnly cookies cannot be cleared from JS anyway — they require the POST/DELETE to /api/session, which logout doesn't call).
findings.evidence: document.cookie = 'fund24-auth=; path=/; max-age=0'

findings.summary: precheck.ts endpoints target API.CHECK (NEXT_PUBLIC_CHECK_API_URL) — worth verifying the precheck worker is still deployed at the same env var that the legacy check endpoints use. If the env var was repurposed, every precheck call fails.
findings.evidence: return apiCall(API.CHECK, '/api/precheck/analyse', …)

findings.summary: app/antraege/[id]/page.tsx is the only place that mutates Antrag dokumente; per 01_pages.json the route is marked AUTH-LEAK-missing-from-PROTECTED_PREFIXES — an unauthenticated user could still trigger the wrappers (Bearer=null) which will hit the worker anyway and likely return 401s, but no client-side guard blocks this first.
findings.evidence: Page imports getAntrag/updateAntragStatus/uploadAntragDokument etc. via fund24.ts; Route is flagged AUTH-LEAK in docs/analysis/full-audit/01_pages.json

findings.summary: Same table name 'users' in TWO different D1 databases with DIFFERENT schemas (zfbf-db: 32 cols, bafa_antraege: 40 cols). Worker routes inconsistently query each. Root of silent data-loss bugs.
findings.evidence: Schema dump shows two distinct 'users' CREATE TABLE DDLs

findings.summary: Three 'favorites' tables across two DBs: zfbf-db.favorites, bafa_antraege.foerdermittel_favoriten, bafa_antraege.me_favoriten. Two wrapper paths (fund24.ts + check.ts) may write to different ones.
findings.evidence: Schema dump

findings.summary: antraege (16 cols) and antraege_v2 (19 cols) coexist in bafa_antraege. Worker uses _v2 but legacy table remains migrated+indexed — dual-write/dual-read risk.
findings.evidence: Both tables present in schema dump

findings.summary: Two audit_logs tables (zfbf-db + bafa_antraege). No single source of truth for compliance/forensics.
findings.evidence: Schema dump

findings.summary: berater_profile (singular, zfbf-db, 11 cols) vs berater_profiles (plural, bafa_antraege, 17 cols). Naming drift.
findings.evidence: Schema dump

findings.summary: Most bafa_antraege tables have 0-1 indexes. unternehmen (34 cols, 0 idx), berater_profiles (17 cols, 0 idx), provisions_modelle, forum_*, rechtsrahmen, kombinationsregeln. Dashboard queries scale linearly.
findings.evidence: Schema dump index counts

findings.summary: 8 foerdermittel_* tables duplicated across zfbf-db AND bafa_antraege (profile, cases, matches, dokumente, funnel_templates, conversations, case_steps, benachrichtigungen). 16 instances total.
findings.evidence: Schema dump

findings.summary: CHECK_DB (foerdermittel-checks) bound to BOTH workers. check_sessions, check_chat, check_ergebnisse written only by worker-check; main worker proxies.
findings.evidence: Both bind database_id=0b2479a3-...

findings.summary: BAFA_CERTS R2 bucket NOT bound. GAP-002 (PR #26) designed but unmerged → berater cannot upload cert.
findings.evidence: Only REPORTS R2 in main wrangler

findings.summary: bafa_antraege.unternehmen (34 cols, 0 idx) — even WHERE user_id = ? is a full scan. Drop-FK migrations (024, 025) removed FKs but didn't add replacement indexes.
findings.evidence: Schema dump

findings.summary: ki_konversationen, ki_usage, ki_verarbeitung stored in bafa_antraege — AI telemetry mixed with user data. Retention unclear.
findings.evidence: Schema dump

findings.summary: rechtsrahmen (19 cols, 0 idx), kombinationsregeln (12 cols, 0 idx), foerder_kombinationen (16 cols, 0 idx) — no caller references. Candidates for orphan-table list.
findings.evidence: Not in Phase-3 endpoint-to-table mapping

findings.summary: call_log, caller_sessions, password_reset_tokens in foerdermittel-checks DB look like leftovers from earlier phone/voice flow.
findings.evidence: No worker handler references

findings.summary: foerderprogramme.foerderprogramme has 73 columns. Scraper populates ~25; rest null. I/O waste on every katalog read.
findings.evidence: Column count in schema dump

findings.summary: Daily backup dumps only DB + BAFA_DB (skips FOERDER_DB, CHECK_DB, BAFA_CONTENT). Restore-from-backup would lose foerderprogramme catalog, check sessions, and learnings.
findings.evidence: performBackup call in scheduled()

findings.summary: ComingSoonBanner over fully functional subtrees — /admin/*, /dashboard/berater/*, /dashboard/unternehmen/{favoriten,tracker,anfragen}, /aktuelles/*, /berater/*. Visible UX friction on features that already work.
PDF export of Fördercheck-Ergebnisse is double-broken (button disabled + onClick shows 'wird in Kürze implementiert' alert).
BAFA-Cert upload flow for Berater designed (PR #26) but not merged. Admin queue is live; berater have no way to put cert into the queue.
JWT_SECRET not documented in frontend .env.example. middleware.ts requires it server-side on Vercel; new onboarding engineer could miss it.
Forum tables (forum_threads, forum_antworten, forum_upvotes, forum_posts) exist across 2 DBs but no UI, no endpoint. Removable.
Kombinationsregeln / foerder_kombinationen / rechtsrahmen — Feature design artefacts never wired to UI or endpoints.
Phone/voice flow leftovers in foerdermittel-checks (call_log, caller_sessions, password_reset_tokens).
Legacy bafa_antraege.antraege — superseded by antraege_v2 but still migrated + indexed.

Dead frontend wrapper exports: getStats, verifyEmail, deleteDokument, deleteAdminNews, fund24.addFavorit variant, berater.listExpertise, berater.listDienstleistungen, unternehmen.getUnternehmen, check.createTrackerVorgang, check.deleteTrackerVorgang.
5 UI component files not imported anywhere: components/ui/{tabs,sheet,separator,dropdown-menu}.tsx, components/shared/EmptyState.tsx. shadcn primitives may be added lazily — keep unless confirmed.
2 unused npm deps: @tailwindcss/typography, @tanstack/react-query-devtools. Remove from package.json.
83 worker endpoints have no frontend caller. ~30 are admin-only + cron-adjacent (kept); rest are candidates for cleanup after manual review.
40 frontend calls match no worker endpoint (after cross-check with worker-check literal grep). Mix of shape-normalisation false positives and genuine mismatches.

8 protected pages are unreachable from any in-app nav (real orphans after Footer/Navbar merge): /dashboard/{berater/profil,berater/tracker,berater/vorlagen,berater/berichte,unternehmen/tracker}, /admin/{audit-logs,email-outbox}, /antraege/[id].
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPPORT_PHONE/_EMAIL/_HOURS planned by PR #24 (support-contact placeholder fix) but not on main and not in .env.example.

recommendedFixOrder: HOUR 1 — Quick wins (≤ 1-line patches): H-P1-01 endpoint URL, H-P1-02 middleware list, H-P1-03/H-P3-05 sitemap shape. Also remove ComingSoonBanner from 5 layouts (G-P5-01).
WEEK 1 — Security hardening: H-P3-01 (auth on /api/check), H-P3-03 (SSRF allowlist), H-P2-01 (DSGVO checkbox split), M-P3-02 (Stripe webhook 4xx on mismatch), L-P4-05 (backup all 5 D1s not just 2).

WEEK 1-2 — Data correctness: H-P1-04 (profil payload), H-P4-01 (favorites consolidation), H-P4-02 (antraege vs antraege_v2 — confirm dual-write absent then drop legacy), H-P4-05 (add idx_unternehmen_user_id etc.).

WEEK 2-4 — Schema consolidation (the deep work): C-P4-01 users table ownership, H-P4-03 audit_logs unification, M-P4-01 foerdermittel_* deduplication, H-P3-06 legacy CHECK worker migration to main worker.
WEEK 4+ — Feature gaps: Finish BAFA-Cert upload (G-P5-03), build PDF export (G-P5-02), decide Forum / Kombinationsregeln keep-or-kill.
